name: Aaron Abke
corePositions: Paul founded gnosticism through Marcion
Protestantism = gnosticism
Docetism = Protestant Christology
Jesus taught love/works/forgiveness only
Paul is the shadow side of Jesus
Theosis through ego crucifixion
James rebuked Paul for 'faith alone'
Paul and James are irreconcilable
Paul was a Herodian 'deep state' agent who deceived early Christians
notablePatterns: Scripture citations are largely accurate, but interpretive frameworks and doctrinal characterizations carry heavy error loads.
Core thesis claims tend to be historically falsifiable and falsified.
Frequently employs Stratagem 11 (Induction with Concealed Counterexamples).
Multi-speaker conversations amplify conspiratorial framing ('deep state,' 'infiltration') absent from solo content.
Cumulative trajectory: Caution (63%) → Unreliable (50%) → Unreliable (59%) → Unreliable (47%) → Unreliable (44%). Cumulative: 48%.

name: Wes Huff
corePositions: NT is the most accurately transmitted ancient document collection
Archaeological artifacts corroborate the New Testament
Manuscript evidence supports biblical reliability
notablePatterns: Core claims about manuscript evidence are well-supported and within academic expertise.
Peripheral factual details (dates, attributions) carry most errors.
Uses cherry-picking of confirming archaeological evidence without addressing complications.
Popular-level lecture format leads to imprecise but not malicious claims.

name: Dr. James Tabor
corePositions: Jesus was a vegetarian
Hebrew Bible contains anti-sacrifice trajectory
Original Christianity was plant-based
Sacrifice texts are interpolations
DSS reveal meaningful textual plurality in Second Temple Judaism
The Masoretic Text is not the only or most original form of the Hebrew Bible
The Shapira scroll deserves reconsideration as potentially authentic
A divine council worldview is embedded in older textual traditions
notablePatterns: Strong command of primary texts and manuscript evidence.
Accuracy improves significantly in survey/bibliographic mode vs. interpretive/argumentative mode (83% vs. 79%).
Tendency to round numbers or give approximate figures when precise data is available.
Core thesis depends on systematic omission of counterevidence in interpretive sessions.
Excellent bibliographic recommendations -- accurate citations of major scholarly works.
Cumulative trajectory: Caution (79%) -> Reliable (83%).

name: Dan McClellan
corePositions: Wes Huff conflates textual criticism with historical criticism
Apologetics masquerades as scholarship on social media
Conservative evangelicals specialize in peripheral fields to avoid critical issues
Gospel-Tiberius comparison is fallacious due to vastly different evidence bases
99% consensus on first-century NT dating is false
notablePatterns: Strong command of scholarly consensus on NT dating, genre, and manuscript evidence.
Occasionally overstates the degree of scholarly unanimity on positions that favor his argument.
Editorial opinions and characterizations of motive sometimes presented as factual claims.
Accurately distinguishes textual criticism from historical criticism — a key methodological point.

name: Metaphysics Mike
corePositions: Biblical Unitarian
Anti-Trinitarian textual criticism
Trinitarian conspiracy thesis
notablePatterns: Inverts scholarly consensus on John 1:18.
Three of seven claimed variants unverifiable from standard critical apparatus.
Methodologically inconsistent — appeals to oldest manuscripts when convenient but argues against them when inconvenient.

name: Nazarene Way Church
corePositions: Jesus was a vegetarian; his movement prohibited flesh consumption
The Passover should be observed without a lamb
Jesus never claimed divinity or divine sonship
The Ebionites/Nazarenes represent Jesus's original movement
Paul's theology is a departure from Jesus's actual teaching
Modern Judaism and Christianity both distort the original message
notablePatterns: Consistent omission of counterevidence from cited sources (Passover lamb, fish miracles, 'son of God' affirmation).
Repeated pejorative labeling of proto-orthodox Christianity ('pagan cult,' 'meth message').
Conflation of distinct scholarly categories (Gospel of Hebrews ≠ Gospel of Nazarenes ≠ Gospel of Ebionites).
Hebrew/Aramaic linguistic errors presented with confidence.
